1.description: Illustration of our model. Akerlof (1970) meets Grossman and Stiglitz (1980).

2.description: Illustration of price informativeness. This figure depicts the degree of price informativeness under large and small arbitrage trading variation givenξ≡XA(θ)+ηz¯, which is a sufficient statistic for net supplyX.

3.description: Illustration of endogenous lemons problem. This figure depicts how a crisis causes a lemons problem.

4.description: Price informativeness effect versus adverse selection effect. This figure depicts fire sales of the high-quality asset.

5.description: Comparative statics. This figure depicts the impact of a reduction in arbitrage capital on the expected price of the high-quality asset and the average quality of the traded asset (thex-axis is scaled as a percentage ofw¯A).

6.description: Comparative statics. This figure depicts the impact of a reduction in arbitrage capital on the circulation rate of the marketable asset (thex-axis is scaled as a percentage ofw¯A).

7.description: Comparative statics. This figure depicts the impact of a reduction in arbitrage capital on the expected price of the high-quality asset under various parameter values (thex-axis is scaled as a percentage ofw¯A).

8.description: Comparative statics. This figure depicts the impact of a reduction in arbitrage capital on economic welfare under various parameter values (thex-axis is scaled as a percentage ofw¯A, and they-axis is scaled as a fraction of the maximum level of the economic welfare measures).

9.description: Comparative statics. This figure depicts the impact of a reduction in arbitrage capital on the expected price of the high-quality asset and the average quality of the traded asset under various assumptions on the margin constraint (thex-axis is scaled as a percentage ofw¯A).
